name: Flight 12
location_str: Orbital Launch Pad 2 @ SpaceX Starbase, USA
description: 12th test flight of the two-stage Starship launch vehicle. Maiden Flight of Starship V3.  The flight test’s primary goal will be to demonstrate each of these new pieces in the flight environment for the first time, with each element of the Starship architecture featuring significant redesigns to enable full and rapid reuse that incorporate learnings from years of development and test.  The Starship upper stage will target multiple in-space and reentry objectives, including a payload deployment of 20 Starlink simulators, similar in size to next-generation Starlink V3 satellites, and two spe
program_description: The SpaceX Starship is a fully reusable super heavy-lift launch vehicle under development by SpaceX since 2012, as a self-funded private spaceflight project.  The second stage of the Starship — is designed as a long-duration cargo and passenger-carrying spacecraft. footer_right: Planned recovery at Gulf of Mexico.
rocket_fact: Astronauts on the International Space Station witness 16 sunrises and sunsets every single day.
